sections.content: SPACE changed the board. W's shape changed - it looks different now. Let me check the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Current nodes:")
for n in seg:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    print(f"  color={n['color']}, rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check top bar
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and min([p[0] for p in n['boundary']]) == 0:
        print(f"  Top bar W: cols {min([p[1] for p in n['boundary']])}-{max([p[1] for p in n['boundary']])}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'c' and min([p[0] for p in n['boundary']]) == 0:
        print(f"  Top bar c: cols {min([p[1] for p in n['boundary']])}-{max([p[1] for p in n['boundary']])}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing SPACE:
- Top bar W went from 3 to 4 pixels (progress!)
- W block moved from cols 29-43 to cols 32-46 (shifted right by 3)
- Red blocks moved to different positions (rows 20-22 and 26-28, cols 29-31)
- N's pixels decreased from 144 to 126 (some of N was consumed/overlapped)
So SPACE seems to:
1. Advance the top bar by 1 pixel
2. Move W right by 3
3. Move red blocks to new positions
4. Consume part of N when overlapping
The goal is likely to fill the entire top bar (32 pixels) by repeatedly pressing SPACE while W overlaps with N. But I need to make sure W stays overlapping with N.
Currently W is at cols 32-46, N is at cols 41-49. They still overlap at cols 41-46. Let me continue pressing SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# SPACE advances top bar and moves W right
# Top bar: 4/32 W pixels
# W at cols 32-46, N at cols 41-49 - still overlapping
# Continue pressing SPACE to fill the bar
# Need 28 more SPACE presses (32 - 4 = 28)

# But W moves right each time, so it might move out of N's range
# N is at cols 41-49, W is at cols 32-46
# After each SPACE, W moves right 3 cols
# After 2 SPACEs: W at cols 38-52, still overlaps N (41-49)
# After 3 SPACEs: W at cols 41-55, still overlaps N (41-49)
# After 4 SPACEs: W at cols 44-58, still overlaps N (41-49) at cols 44-49
# After 5 SPACEs: W at cols 47-61, still overlaps N (41-49) at cols 47-49
# After 6 SPACEs: W at cols 50-64, no longer overlaps N

# So I can press SPACE about 5-6 more times before W moves out of range
# Let me press SPACE 5 times and check
predict(expect_change=True, note='Press SPACE 5 times to advance top bar')
action(['SPACE', 'SPACE', 'SPACE', 'SPACE', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
